properties.titulo_evento: Unfazed by Iran’s threats, Bulgaria moves to aid U.S. military operations
properties.descricao: A Bulgarian parliamentary committee has approved a U.S. request to let American forces use a local air base to support the U.S. war against Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: Backed by France, Macron sees Germany as nuclear-deterrence ‘vanguard’ in Europe
properties.descricao: A French nuclear-capable Rafale jet had been temporarily hosted at a German air base − specifically at Nörvenich near Cologne.

properties.titulo_evento: Cost of Iran war rises to $37.5 billion, Pentagon requests $67.1 billion more
properties.descricao: The Iran war has cost the DoD $37.5 billion to date, Hegseth told senators Tuesday, as he requested $67.1 billion more in emergency funding.

properties.titulo_evento: New British prime minister gains early defense win with Canada fighter jet tie-up
properties.descricao: Canada has joined the GCAP fighter program as an observer, marking the first expansion beyond the founding members Britain, Italy and Japan.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran warns Bulgaria against aiding US military operations
properties.descricao: Iran’s foreign ministry spokesman warned Bulgaria on Tuesday against allowing the U.S. to use its territory for military operations against Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany to study French cooperation offer on SAMP/T air-defense system
properties.descricao: German government leaders will study an offer by French President Emmanuel Macron to cooperate on the French-Italian SAMP/T air defense system.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia may use captured Ukrainian drones against Poland and Baltics, Warsaw warns
properties.descricao: Russia has captured a large number of Ukrainian drones and may be planning to use them against NATO or to stage a false flag attack, Polish officials said.

properties.titulo_evento: Doodle Labs eyes US, European production sites as demand soars for jam-resistant drones
properties.descricao: Doodle Labs hopes to turn its success in equipping jam-hardened drones in Ukraine into additional production sites in Europe and he United States.

properties.titulo_evento: Israel’s IAI unveils ‘Hypnosis’ system for confusing enemy drone swarms
properties.descricao: The system, called Hypnosis, consists of an array of mobile “stations” around a defended area that send out jamming signals against drone swarms.

properties.titulo_evento: Baulking at shipping sanctions, Greece balances Ukraine aid, fear of Russia
properties.descricao: Sanctions on Russian LNG would hurt Greek shipping, argues Athens, but it also fears Moscow’s hybrid warfare.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine hits more warehouses of top Russian online retailer
properties.descricao: Ukrainian drones have hit two more warehouses belonging to Russia’s biggest online retailer Wildberries.

properties.titulo_evento: America’s decades-old bipartisan consensus on Israel has been shattered
properties.descricao: The Democratic establishment has faced growing pressure to change its stance on Israel. It has finally begun to do so.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia reports injuries after top online retailer Wildberries hit again
properties.descricao: Company CEO says logistics centres in Krasnodar and Nevinnomyssk evacuated days after workers killed in earlier attack.

properties.titulo_evento: Ossoff challenges Hegseth on cost and duration of Iran war
properties.descricao: Ossoff challenges Hegseth on cost and duration of Iran war

properties.titulo_evento: Iran, Gulf hit as Hormuz, Bab al-Mandeb close: What’s the latest?
properties.descricao: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth tells US senators that the war has cost the US $37.5bn so far.

properties.titulo_evento: Rabat demands answers after Moroccan man dies during arrest in Italy
properties.descricao: Italian Prime Minister Meloni says truth must be 'pursued to the very end' after death of Abderrahim Fakir in Bologna.

properties.titulo_evento: US ‘open’ to negotiations with Iran but Tehran not ‘serious’, Rubio says
properties.descricao: US Secretary of State says Iranian control over Strait of Hormuz sets 'dangerous precedent', as attacks continue.

properties.titulo_evento: Air defences activate in Iran and flames rise from US attacks
properties.descricao: Explosions could be heard over Tehran as the US army said it had completed its 11th consecutive day of strikes on Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: Hegseth called a ‘failure’ over Iran as he reveals $37b cost of war
properties.descricao: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth gets into a heated exchange after being called a failed leader by Michigan Senator.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war has cost US $37.5bn so far, Hegseth says, as Pentagon seeks billions more
properties.descricao: The hearing was disrupted by protests and a shouting match as Hegseth testified at a pivotal time in the war.

properties.titulo_evento: US renews strikes on Iran as Trump threatens to attack underground nuclear site
properties.descricao: The US president's threat to target a key Iranian nuclear site came ahead of the 11th consecutive night of attacks.

properties.titulo_evento: Why Iran is risking so much over the Strait of Hormuz
properties.descricao: None of the choices facing Iran’s leaders offer a clear path to victory, as the conflict with the US continues.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: Prosecutors search Deutsche Bank headquarters
properties.descricao: Officials are reportedly investigating fraudulent tax claims made by the bank's subsidiary Postbank between 2008 and 2010. Meanwhile, the story of Timmy the Whale continues with new DNA results. Follow DW for more.

properties.titulo_evento: Will surge in German business bankruptcies hit growth?
properties.descricao: Company insolvencies in Germany are rising sharply. Is this a sign of deeper economic weakness, or just a market correction that could ultimately benefit future growth?

properties.titulo_evento: France: Environment minister quits over pesticide bill
properties.descricao: Monique Barbut submitted her resignation in protest over the reintroduction of pesticides that scientists say harm human health as well as bee populations. But President Emmanuel Macron reportedly refused to accept it.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine renews strikes on Russia's largest online retailer
properties.descricao: Ukraine's military has again targeted warehouses belonging to Wildberries. The company, which Kyiv says provides logistics for Russia's military, was previously targeted on Saturday.

properties.titulo_evento: Hegseth says Iran war has cost $37.5 billion so far
properties.descricao: US Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth is also pushing for more funding for the war. It comes as the US military struck Iran for the 11th consecutive night. DW has the latest.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires military chief after days of protests
properties.descricao: Mass protests in recent days in Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities had called for the removal of commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi. Volodymyr Zelenskyy has named Mykhailo Drapatyi as the army's new chief.

properties.titulo_evento: Jordan faces growing risk of becoming latest frontline state in US-Iran conflict
properties.descricao: As Iranian attacks intensify, Jordan is emerging as the latest frontline state in the widening Middle East conflict. Why is the Hashemite Kingdom a target for Tehran?

properties.titulo_evento: Fact check: Trump wasn't edited out of Spain World Cup photo
properties.descricao: After Spain won the FIFA World Cup, the Spanish Football Federation posted a photo of the trophy celebrations, raising questions about whether Donald Trump had been edited out. We took a closer look.

properties.titulo_evento: Is Germany investing in the right weapons?
properties.descricao: Germany wants to build up its army and pour hundreds of millions of euros into its defenses. But in a world where the battlefield is increasingly dominated by cheap drones, how many expensive tanks does the country need?

properties.titulo_evento: Poll: Trump’s MAGA base is souring on his Iran war
properties.descricao: A July POLITICO Poll shows the share of MAGA voters who think the war is worth the economic cost has fallen to roughly over a third, down from 50 percent in May.

properties.titulo_evento: The EU’s Russia sanctions crunch
properties.descricao: Today is a make-or-break moment for the EU’s 21st sanctions package targeting Russia. After EU countries failed to agree on the ins and outs of the agreement last week, ambassadors are meeting again today in Brussels to finally clinch the deal. ...

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine’s drone strikes pile pressure on Russian economy
properties.descricao: As its refineries and warehouses burn, Russia's financial markets are starting to freeze.

properties.titulo_evento: Post-Orbán sanctions showdown exposes EU capitals’ red lines
properties.descricao: With Budapest no longer providing cover, EU governments are being forced to own their objections to tougher action against Russia.

properties.titulo_evento: Global Oil Price Vaults Over $95 a Barrel as War Threats Intensify
properties.descricao: The risk of continued fighting and a threatened blockade in the Red Sea added to uncertainty about the flow of energy from the region.

properties.titulo_evento: See How Houthis Put the Red Sea at Risk as an Alternative Oil Route
properties.descricao: Saudi Arabia has diverted huge amounts of oil to the Red Sea since the Iran war began, but now the Iranian-backed Houthi militants say that they intend to block that route.

properties.titulo_evento: Houthis Threaten Red Sea Blockade, Putting Oil Market at Greater Risk
properties.descricao: With the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, the militant group’s threats in the Red Sea jeopardize Saudi Arabia’s main workaround for oil exports.

properties.titulo_evento: Oil Buyers Battered by the Iran War Energy Crisis Race to Build Buffers
properties.descricao: As rising fuel costs clobber their economies, the Philippines and other developing countries in Asia are pushing to build strategic petroleum reserves to cushion against future shocks.

properties.titulo_evento: Andy Burnham, New UK Prime Minister, Names John Healey as Top Finance Official
properties.descricao: As chancellor, John Healey, who quit as defense secretary in the former government, will fill one of the most consequential posts, overseeing fiscal and economic policy.
